title: SACRAMENTO
prompt_preview:
[1 chunks] Task: Extract all distinct claims about AI or emerging technology in this press release. Do NOT assume a fixed number.
Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem

  "claims": [
    "AB 2208 is the most comprehensive legislation in the US aimed at phasing out the sale of mercury-containing fluorescent bulbs and lamps.",
    "Fluorescent lamps contain mercury, a potent toxin that can cause serious and permanent damage to the central nervous system.",
raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "AB 2208 is the most comprehensive legislation in the US aimed at phasing out the sale of mercury-containing fluorescent bulbs and lamps.",
    "Fluorescent lamps contain mercury, a potent toxin that can cause serious and permanent damage to the central nervous system.",
    "The effects of mercury exposure are especially pronounced in children and other vulnerable populations.",
    "Fluorescent lamps are categorized as hazardous waste by the state, but many are still thrown away in the trash.",
    "Improper disposal of fluorescent lamps can lead to contamination of regular garbage and expose solid waste workers to toxic mercury.",
    "LEDs are on average 50% more efficient than fluorescent lamps.",
    "LEDs last 2-3 times longer than fluorescent lamps.",
    "Switching from fluorescent to LED could save a small office $6,000 over the typical lifetime of LED lamps.",
    "A school could save as much as $24,000 by switching from fluorescent to LED lighting.",
    "Disposing of an LED is a much safer and cheaper process compared to disposing of fluorescent lamps.",
    "Switching to LEDs by the year 2030 could result in a decrease of upwards of 957,000 metric tons of CO2 emissions.",
    "The reduction in CO2 emissions from switching to LEDs is equivalent to removing 206,000 gas-powered passenger vehicles from the roads annually.",
    "The State of Vermont has recognized the need to phase out compact fluorescent lamps by 2024.",
    "The European Union announced it will phase out all general-use fluorescent lamps by September 2023."
  ]
}
```
